beats.caption: AC-1 - new session defaults to orchestrated (Joe)
beats.expectedScreen: the Dev Env 'New session' modal open, with an 'Orchestrator' dropdown whose selected value reads 'Joe - dev (default)'

segments.caption: AC-2 - modes gary / joe / james offered by name
segments.expectedScreen: session-request.ts MODE_OPTIONS listing value 'joe' (Joe - dev), 'gary' (Gary - assistant), 'james' (James - product / architect), plus 'plain'; DEFAULT_MODE = 'joe'

highlight.note: gary / joe / james: the orchestrated faces the New-session dropdown offers by name (DEFAULT_MODE = joe)

beats.caption: AC-3 - plain escape hatch, labeled for debugging Garrison itself
beats.expectedScreen: the Orchestrator dropdown now showing the plain option label 'Plain claude, for debugging Garrison itself (unorchestrated, logged)'

segments.command: npx tsx -e 'import {buildSessionRequest} from "./fittings/seed/dev-env/ui/session-request.ts"; console.log(JSON.stringify({ "default (no flag)": buildSessionRequest({path:"/x", mode:null}), joe: buildSessionRequest({path:"/x", mode:"joe"}), plain: buildSessionRequest({path:"/x", mode:"plain"}) }, null, 2))'
segments.caption: AC-4 - selecting plain yields request { plain: true }
segments.expectedScreen: JSON output of buildSessionRequest: the plain case is { "path": "/x", "plain": true }; the default and joe cases carry no plain flag

highlight.note: the plain escape hatch sets plain:true; default (null) and joe stay orchestrated (no plain flag)

segments.caption: AC-5 - server logs plain-session use
segments.expectedScreen: server.mjs handleCreateSession: when body.plain is true it logs '[dev-env] PLAIN session requested ... (unorchestrated escape hatch, D22)'

highlight.note: every plain (unorchestrated) session is logged server-side - the escape hatch is auditable
